title: Essential Cooking Terminology
content:
ESSENTIAL TERMINOLOGY

BATTER TYPES
Pour Batter: 1 part flour to 1 part liquid
Soft Drop Batter: 1¼-2 parts flour to 1 part liquid
Medium Batter: 3 parts flour to 1 part liquid
Stiff Drop Batter: 4 parts flour to 1 part liquid

DOUGH TYPES
Soft/Roll Dough: Stiff enough to roll on lightly floured board. Chill thoroughly for easier handling.
Stiff Dough: Stiff enough to knead without sticking to lightly floured board.

PREPARATION TERMS
Grating: Finely dividing food by rubbing over metal surface with sharp projections
Grinding: Finely dividing food through food chopper
Blanching: Cooking briefly in boiling water (3-5 minutes)
Chopping: Finely dividing into small, irregular pieces
Cubing/Dicing: Cutting into uniform small pieces
Mincing: Finely dividing with sharp knife or scissors
Shredding: Cutting into long thin strips
Slicing: Cutting into broad, thin pieces
Searing: Exposing to very high temperature until outer surface cooks

MIXING TECHNIQUES
Beating: Rapid over-and-over motion to remove lumps and incorporate air
Folding: Slow motion down through mixture then up and over to prevent air escape
Whipping: Rapid beating to incorporate large volume of air
Stirring/Blending: Circular motion to combine ingredients and prevent sticking
Marinating: Allowing food to stand in seasoned liquid until well flavored
Egging and Crumbing: Dipping molded food into beaten egg, then crumbs, then egg again before frying
